articles.title: The Inner Workings of JavaScript Source Maps
articles.description: Ever wondered how devtools can magically turn mangled, minified JavaScript back into readable source while debugging? Zero magic; that’s a source map doing its job. But how do source maps actually work under the hood?
articles.author: Manoj Vivek

articles.title: Anders Hejlsberg's Take on the Rise of TypeScript
articles.description: The co-creator of TypeScript says he’s ‘floored’ by the language’s success (now the number one language on GitHub in 2025), shares a little info on the on-going compiler rewrite in Go, and the increasing role of AI.
articles.author: The GitHub Blog

articles.title: Embedding TypeScript with Hako
articles.description: A technical look at the Hako JavaScript engine. It runs in WebAssembly so can be more easily embedded in more environments, including mobile apps.
articles.author: Andrew Sampson

articles.title: ▶  The Talk Videos from CascadiaJS 2025
articles.description: CascadiaJS took place a month ago and the talk videos have been gradually rolling out onto YouTube. You can learn more about TanStack with Jack Herrington, the origin story of JavaScript with Annie Sexton, the Web Monetization API with Ioana Chiorean, and more.
articles.author: CascadiaJS

articles.title: High-Performance Syntax Highlighting with the CSS Custom Highlight API
articles.description: Now supported in all major browsers, the CSS Custom Highlight API provides a mechanism for styling arbitrary text ranges in a document by using JavaScript to create the ranges, and CSS to style them.
articles.author: Pavitra Golchha

articles.title: How to Throttle Specific Requests in Chrome DevTools
articles.description: Chrome DevTools has supported network throttling for a long time but you can now throttle requests to specific URLs or domains too, which could be ideal if you want to see how your site handles the failure of third party scripts.
articles.author: Matt Zeunert

articles.title: ?! Importing Node Modules from BitTorrent
articles.description: A fascinating demo of the power of Node.js’s customization hooks for overriding import behavior.
articles.author: Evan Hahn

articles.title: Advanced Beginner’s Guide to ClojureScript
articles.description: An introduction to a compiler for the functional Clojure Lisp dialect that produces JavaScript.
articles.author: Roman Liutikov

articles.title: Perspective 4.0: High Performance Analytics and Data Visualization Component
articles.description: Originally built by JP Morgan, this data visualization component, built in C++ and compiled to WebAssembly, is well-suited for large and real-time streaming datasets. The demo on the homepage lets you try visualization types at up to 1000 changes per second. v4.0 sees the project move to the OpenJS Foundation.
articles.author: OpenJS Foundation

articles.title: Vue Data UI 3.6: Vue Components Library for Data Storytelling
articles.description: There’s a lot packed into this suite of components, including basic chart types through to donuts, sparklines, world views, stacks, heatmaps, word clouds, and more. Definitely worth a look if you’re using Vue. Live examples.
articles.author: Alec Lloyd Probert

articles.title: 🖼️ image-dimensions: Get the Dimensions of Images
articles.description: A zero-dependency way to get the pixel width and height of JPEG, PNG/APNG, GIF, WebP, AVIF and HEIF images in any modern JavaScript environment.
articles.author: Sindre Sorhus

articles.title: React Syntax Highlighter: A Code Syntax Highlighting Component
articles.description: If you need to show off source code in your React app somewhere, this is a component that does the job. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Conor Hastings

articles.title: CSSOM: A CSS Parser Written in Pure JavaScript
articles.description: Also a partial implementation of CSS Object Model.
articles.author: Nikita Vasilyev

articles.description: GitHub has updated its post about ongoing npm security changes. npm classic tokens can no longer be created, but existing ones will continue to work till February 3, 2026 at the latest.
I enjoyed Lea Verou's cute proxy-based trick for creating symbols on demand.
The Svelte team has a November 2025 update on what's new in Svelte.
🇷🇴 The popular JSHeroes conference is taking place in Romania next May 14-15. If you'd like to speak, its CFP is open until December 31.
Storybook 10 – The popular frontend component workshop is now ESM-only, lighter, has a new module automocking system, Vitest 4 support, and more.
htmx 4.0 Alpha 1 – There was never going to be a v3 of htmx, so how about v4? Carson Gross tells the full story here.
React Email 5.0 – Suite of components for creating emails in React. Now with a dark mode switcher and Tailwind 4 support.
Turborepo 2.6, Video.js v10, ESLint Config Inspector v1.4

articles.title: The Web Animation Performance Tier List
articles.description: A comprehensive and detailed guide in which Matt shares everything he knows about animation performance, covering the render pipeline, hardware acceleration, and more. Animations are ranked in a tier list, noting how various techniques impact things. A must read if you’re striving for a smooth, responsive UI.
articles.author: Matt Perry

articles.title: High-Performance Syntax Highlighting with the CSS Custom Highlight API
articles.description: Now supported in all major browsers, the CSS Custom Highlight API provides a mechanism for styling arbitrary text ranges in a document by using JavaScript to create the ranges, and CSS to style them.
articles.author: Pavitra Golchha

articles.title: The Weird Parts of position: sticky;
articles.description: A highly useful, but often tricky CSS feature to get 100% right. Adam runs through how it works, and highlights how to tackle some common points of frustration.
articles.author: Adam Rackis

articles.title: pathLength Makes SVG Path Animations Easier to Manage
articles.description: A quick tip on how to combine stroke-dasharray, stroke-dashoffset and CSS transitions to effectively animate an SVG path.
articles.author: Stefan Judis

articles.title: Perfectly Pointed Tooltips: To the Corners
articles.description: We shared the first two parts of this series on tooltips last week, and here’s the latest, this time focusing on perfecting the corners.
articles.author: Temani Afif

articles.title: Programming Principles for Self Taught Frontend Developers
articles.description: Some solid, grounded, actionable rule-of-thumb suggestions that should straighten up or improve your workflow.
articles.author: Kilian Valkhof

articles.title: Effectively Monitoring Web Performance
articles.description: Outlines a strategy for web perf optimization and explains the roles that different types of data play in it.
articles.author: Matt Zeunert

articles.title: “A.I.” Browsers: The Price of Admission Is Too High
articles.description: “If data is the fuel for A.I., what is the product?”
articles.author: Bruce Lawson Opinion

articles.title: Umami 3.0: Self-Hosted, Privacy-Focused Google Analytics Alternative
articles.description: A self-hosting alternative to the likes of Plausible and Google Analytics. Here’s a full feature list. It's also available as a paid hosted service.
articles.author: Umami Software, Inc.

articles.title: Travels: A Fast, Framework-Agnostic Undo/Redo Library
articles.description: Allows you to add undo/redo functionality to apps like text editors, drawing tools, or other interactive software. Boasts better memory management by only storing changes, rather than full snapshots.
articles.author: mutative

articles.title: FileMock: A Free Test File Generator to Add Test Data to Your Apps
articles.description: Allows you to generate videos, audio clips, images, and documents in dozens of formats and customizable to fit your testing needs.
articles.author: FileMock

articles.title: sitemap.js 9.0: Sitemap Generation CLI Tool and Node.js Library
articles.description: XML ‘sitemap’ files help search spiders (e.g. Googlebot) crawl your site and you can offload the boring task of generating them to this library/CLI if your current system doesn't produce them for you.
articles.author: Eugene Kalinin

articles.title: Horizon: The Current Sky at Your Approximate Location, Rendered as a CSS Gradient
articles.description: In other words, if you view it at night it will just be a black page! 😅 You can see the result here when the sun is out. In old school fashion, it uses meta http-equiv="Refresh" (a trick Drudge Report still uses to this day) to stay up to date and is rendered server-side. We dig it.
articles.author: Daniel Lazaro

articles.title: The Scope Creep
articles.description: An amusing ‘choose-your-own-adventure’ style interactive narrative game. You assume the role of a project manager tasked with delivering a 'cursed' website brief for one of your clients. You’ve got to navigate the project phases, make choices, and face the inevitable issues.
articles.author: Wondermake
